questions.question: Why freeze the task record schema before writing scoring code?
options: It makes the JSON parser run faster
It locks the contract so metrics, runners, and post-processors agree on field names
It removes the need for a validator
It allows arbitrary new metric names without further changes
questions.explanation: The schema is the contract every downstream consumer reads from. Locking it lets you change components independently without breaking the rest.

questions.question: What is the closed vocabulary that the metric_name field accepts?
options: Whatever the runner is configured to recognise
exact_match, f1, bleu_4, rouge_l, accuracy, code_exec
Any string the task author chooses
Only exact_match and f1
questions.explanation: The lesson pins the metric vocabulary to six names. Adding one requires a new lesson and a new validator entry.

questions.question: What does the validator do when it sees an unknown top-level field on a record?
options: Strips it and continues
Logs a warning but accepts the record
Returns a validation error and rejects the record
Renames it to metadata.unknown
questions.explanation: Unknown fields are a forward-compatibility hazard. The validator rejects them so the schema stays the only source of truth.

questions.question: Why are few-shot examples attached to the task record rather than computed by the runner?
options: The runner does not have file I/O
Author intent is part of the eval; binding examples to the task removes per-model variance in prompt construction
这样可以节省磁盘空间
Few-shot is only used during fine-tuning
questions.explanation: Few-shot composition is part of the eval definition, not the model interface. Putting it in the task means every model sees the same prompt.

questions.question: Which category-metric pair is illegal under the validator?
options: summary + rouge_l
arithmetic + exact_match
mcq + bleu_4
code_exec + code_exec
questions.explanation: BLEU on a single letter is meaningless. The validator enforces that mcq tasks use exact_match or accuracy only.

questions.question: 当生成内容为 `Answer: C is correct` 时，post_process 规则 extract_letter 返回什么？
options: 完整的字符串保持不变
C
Answer
一个空字符串
questions.explanation: extract_letter 返回生成内容中第一个匹配 [A-E] 的字符。该字符串在主体开头处有 C。
